theme: The 5AM call that saved a 120-person event

linkedin.text:
The 5AM call that saved a 120-person event.

Seven years ago. Barcelona. 120 people from one of Israel's largest retail chains. Day 2 of 4.

My phone rings at 5AM. It's our local coordinator in Barcelona.

"The hotel has overbooked. They're moving 23 rooms to another hotel. Including all of the executive team."

You have two choices in that moment: panic, or solve.

Here's what happened in the next 90 minutes:

5:00 AM — I called the hotel GM directly. He didn't answer. I called again.
5:10 AM — I called the hotel's owning group emergency line.
5:20 AM — Hotel GM called back. I presented the contract breach.
5:30 AM — Negotiation: we get to keep all our rooms, they move their regular guests.
5:45 AM — Our teams booked alternative rooms in 2 backup hotels (that we had already identified as contingency 3 weeks earlier).
6:15 AM — I walked into the lobby to personally greet the early risers. No one knew there had been a problem.

This is why you hire experienced event producers, not just event coordinators.

The difference: experience means you've made the 3AM calls before. You know who to call. You know what to say.

Have you ever had an event crisis that required this kind of intervention?

#EventProduction #BehindTheScenes #CorporateEvents #Crisis #EventProfessionals #UproductionEvents #MICE #IncentiveTravel #EventPlanning #ExperienceMatter

instagram.text:
5AM. 120 people. Barcelona. The hotel just overbooked our entire executive team. 🔴

How it got fixed before anyone woke up:

5:00 — Called the hotel GM. No answer. Called again.
5:20 — Reached the owning group's emergency line.
5:30 — Contract breach negotiation. We keep every room.
5:45 — Backup hotels activated (we'd prepped them 3 weeks earlier).
6:15 — Standing in the lobby. Smiling. Greeting guests. No one ever knew.

Experience = knowing exactly who to call at 5AM.

📌 Save this and tag the planner who'll appreciate it.

facebook.text:
Seven years ago my phone rang at 5AM. Barcelona, day 2 of 4, 120 people from one of Israel's largest retail chains. 🔴

Our local coordinator: "The hotel has overbooked. They're moving 23 rooms to another hotel — including the entire executive team."

In that moment you have two choices: panic, or solve.

So I called the hotel GM. No answer. Called again. Then the owning group's emergency line. When the GM finally called back, I walked him through the contract breach calmly, and we negotiated: we keep every one of our rooms, they move their regular guests instead. The backup hotels we'd quietly lined up three weeks earlier never even had to be used.

By 6:15 I was standing in the lobby greeting the early risers with a coffee. Not one guest ever knew anything had gone wrong.

That's the real difference between an event coordinator and an event producer — experience means you've already made the 5AM calls, and you know exactly who to dial.

Ever had an event crisis that needed this kind of save? Tell us in the comments. 💬

#EventProduction #BehindTheScenes #CorporateEvents #MICE
